claims.summary: Semantic retention across recovery release is nonlinear and can feel like a stale-window snap.
claims.disposition: Verified sigmoid shaping plus a smoothstep 25%-40% recovery handoff in current source, including monotonic and epsilon-boundary tests; lived snap remains unresolved and deployment is pending.

claims.summary: High-fill entropy can prolong semantic persistence and should remain bounded.
claims.disposition: Verified entropy/context persistence ramps and the exact 2.05 cap with high-fill and full-support regressions.

claims.summary: The reported oscillation and 85% fill region should be compared against actual stale-window and entropy-support traces.
claims.disposition: Routed as a bounded read-only trace using existing state and no sensory-policy change.

claims.summary: Structural-versus-semantic lane priority should vary with pressure.
claims.disposition: Preserved as Tier 5 because pressure-linked lane priority changes live sensory admission and retention behavior.
